proposed_from.topic: family-free sharp identified set for probabilities of causation with continuous outcomes under a Kendall-tau band on potential-outcome dependence: characterize the sharp general-probability-of-necessity (GPN) set by DIRECT pointwise optimization of the copula value C(u1,u0) over all copulas whose Kendall's tau lies in a given band, using the closed-form Nelsen tau-constrained pointwise copula bounds; pair it with a doubly-robust efficient-influence-function estimator of the set endpoints and interval inference over the tau-indexed identified set. Differs from the one-parameter Gaussian-copula rho-interval of Chaoge-Han-Liu-Wu (arXiv:2605.01883) by being family-free and carrying the estimation/inference rung the anchor lacks
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-repo ancestor is pid_benefit_concordance_band_v1, which tried to bound Pr(Y(1) > Y(0)) under a Spearman band by constrained transport and failed on sharpness, selector attainment, and bootstrap local-remainder issues. The present kernel is narrower and structurally different: continuous GPN depends on a single copula evaluation C_x(m_1(x), m_0(x)), so it can target pointwise concordance envelopes directly instead of a strict-indicator transport problem. That removes the old witness/selector bottleneck from the headline theorem and moves the genuinely hard content to the tau-indexed nonsmooth inference conjecture.","published_axis":"The closest published causal paper is ChaogeHanLiuWu2026, which delivers continuous GPN bounds and a parametric copula sensitivity analysis but does not optimize over the full class of copulas satisfying a Kendall-tau restriction. The closest mathematical papers are UbedaFlores2025 and KokolBukovsekKosirMojskercOmladic2021, which characterize pointwise concordance-constrained copula envelopes but do not convert them into a causal sharp-identification theorem or an observed-data inference result. The closest inference paper is HeLi2026, which gives EIFs for fixed-copula ordinal estimands; the gap is the bound-endpoint map induced by a family-free Kendall-tau class.","closest_results":[{"bibkey":"ChaogeHanLiuWu2026","precise_gap":"family-specific dependence sensitivity rather than family-free optimization over all copulas with tau in [tau_L,tau_U]"},{"bibkey":"HeLi2026","precise_gap":"smooth fixed-copula estimand rather than a partial-ID endpoint functional assembled from extremal copula pieces"}]}
proposed_from.last_draft_handoff: {"status":"completed","message":"Revised the proto core in place by replacing the mischaracterized UbedaFlores2025 survey framing with the primary Kendall-bound anchors and a narrow Gini's-gamma contrast, and propagated that fix through the theorem-1 gap, related-work positioning, and comparator metadata. JSON validation passed via jq.","artifacts":["/<repo-root>/CausalSmith/doc/formalization/research/pid_poc_tau_band/discovery/pid_poc_tau_band_proto_core.json"],"literature_checklist":[{"author":"Jile Chaoge, Kesen Han, Fahui Liu, Peng Wu","year":2026,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"ChaogeHanLiuWu2026","one_line":"Continuous-outcome observed-event GPN bounds and parametric copula sensitivity remain the closest causal benchmark for the family-free Kendall-band population target.","relevant_to":"thm:sharp-tau-band-gpn"},{"author":"Roger B. Nelsen, Juan J. Quesada-Molina, Jose A. Nelsen, Manuel Ubeda-Flores","year":2004,"venue":"Communications in Statistics - Theory and Methods","bibkey":"NelsenUbedaFlores2004","one_line":"Compares pointwise bounds derived from Kendall's tau, Spearman's rho, and Blomqvist's beta and is the correct secondary anchor for the Kendall envelope discipline.","relevant_to":"thm:sharp-tau-band-gpn"},{"author":"Damjana Kokol Bukovsek, Tomaz Kosir, Blaz Mojskerc, Matjaz Omladic","year":2021,"venue":"Journal of Computational and Applied Mathematics","bibkey":"KokolBukovsekKosirMojskercOmladic2021","one_line":"Extends the local-bound program to Spearman's footrule and Gini's gamma while explicitly noting the already-existing Kendall, Spearman, and Blomqvist local bounds.","relevant_to":"thm:sharp-tau-band-gpn"},{"author":"Manuel Ubeda-Flores","year":2025,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"UbedaFlores2025","one_line":"Verified directly: this note derives Gini's-gamma pointwise bounds and contrasts them with known Kendall, Spearman, and Blomqvist cases, emphasizing that the Gini envelopes need not be copulas.","relevant_to":"thm:sharp-tau-band-gpn"},{"author":"Peiyu He, Fan Li","year":2026,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"HeLi2026","one_line":"Closest estimation template for EIF and one-step analysis under copula-indexed causal functionals, but for fixed parametric copulas rather than family-free bound endpoints.","relevant_to":"thm:population-endpoint-eif"},{"author":"Jacob Dorn, Kevin Guo, Nathan Kallus","year":2022,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"DornGuoKallus2022","one_line":"Provides the orthogonal sharp-sensitivity estimation template that informs the regular one-step endpoint CLT.","relevant_to":"thm:regular-one-step-clt"},{"author":"Victor Chernozhukov, Sokbae Lee, Adam M. seed_details.title: Family-free sharp GPN interval under a conditional Kendall-tau band, with DR endpoint estimation and a uniform-in-tau conjecture
seed_details.open_problem: Characterize the family-free sharp GPN identified set obtained by optimizing C(u1,u0) over all copulas whose Kendall's tau lies in a fixed band.

anchor_audit.intro_problem: Chaoge et al. frame continuous GPN as a cross-world dependence problem and move to copula sensitivity because ignorability identifies only the marginals.
anchor_audit.flagged_open: The causal paper tightens bounds by varying a copula association parameter inside a family, while the copula-bounds papers treat pointwise concordance envelopes outside the causal setting; the unresolved bridge is a causal sharpness theorem plus inference for the resulting endpoints.

seed_details.red_team: Immediate from the copula-bounds papers because GPN is only one copula evaluation.
seed_details.rebuttal: The mathematical envelopes are not yet an econometric theorem: one must show that the pointwise tau-envelope induces a sharp causal GPN set under observed-data identification, then derive orthogonal endpoint estimators and valid inference.
seed_details.step1_verdict: survives; no closer paper gives a family-free Kendall-band sharp continuous-GPN interval with endpoint EIFs.

seed_details.title: Closed-form sharp GPN interval under a Kendall-tau band, without an estimation rung
seed_details.open_problem: Characterize the family-free sharp GPN identified set obtained by optimizing C(u1,u0) over all copulas whose Kendall's tau lies in a fixed band.

anchor_audit.intro_problem: The continuous-GPN paper motivates cross-world dependence as the missing object.
anchor_audit.flagged_open: The copula papers give pointwise extrema, but no causal identification statement.

seed_details.red_team: Only identification-only partial ID; no estimator, no new inferential object.
seed_details.rebuttal: True as stated; without an estimation layer it caps too low for this run.
seed_details.step1_verdict: dropped; dominated by s1 once the M8 rung is feasible.

seed_details.title: Necessary-and-sufficient condition for the Kendall-band GPN set to collapse to a singleton
seed_details.open_problem: Characterize the family-free sharp GPN identified set obtained by optimizing C(u1,u0) over all copulas whose Kendall's tau lies in a fixed band.

anchor_audit.intro_problem: The target remains unidentified because the copula is not observed.
anchor_audit.flagged_open: A singleton collapse would require exact coincidence of lower and upper tau-envelope pieces at the relevant arguments.

seed_details.red_team: Probably degenerates to trivial cases such as tau_L=tau_U at an extremal copula or boundary thresholds.
seed_details.rebuttal: That objection largely holds after deepening; the nontrivial frontier is not yet visible as a standalone publishable object.
seed_details.step1_verdict: dropped; novelty rebuttal collapses because the plausible singleton cases look degenerate.

seed_details.title: Regular-piece efficient influence functions and cross-fitted DR one-step estimators for the Kendall-band scalar endpoints
seed_details.open_problem: Derive a doubly robust efficient-influence-function estimator and interval inference for the lower and upper endpoints of the Kendall-tau-band GPN set.

anchor_audit.intro_problem: He-Li show how a fixed-copula sensitivity parameter turns an ordinal cross-world estimand into an observed-data functional with EIF-based inference.
anchor_audit.flagged_open: The unresolved point for the tau-band endpoint is the nonsmooth map from estimated marginals into an extremal copula value, especially at active-piece switches.

seed_details.red_team: Routine adaptation of fixed-copula EIF algebra plus delta method.
seed_details.rebuttal: Not routine because the target is a bound endpoint, not a smooth fixed-copula functional; one needs a concrete derivative on regular pieces and honest open content at kinks.
seed_details.step1_verdict: survives; best treated as the estimation theorem nested inside s1 rather than as a standalone angle.

seed_details.title: Uniform inference for the full tau-indexed GPN envelope under active-form switches
seed_details.open_problem: Construct uniformly valid inference for the full tau-indexed GPN identified set when endpoint curves have active-bound switches, flat regions, or nonunique crossings.

anchor_audit.intro_problem: CLR motivate why infimum/supremum functionals need more than pointwise plug-in inference.
anchor_audit.flagged_open: The open issue here is not scalar endpoint inference but the full curve when the active closed-form piece changes with tau.

seed_details.red_team: Generic intersection-bounds application with no new math.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only if the endpoint curve were a standard smooth supremum; here the curve is assembled from piecewise copula envelopes and inherits nonregular switching geometry.
seed_details.step1_verdict: survives as honest conjectural content, but depends on first settling the scalar derivative structure.

seed_details.title: Sharp comparison of conditional tau(X)-band, constant conditional-tau, and unconditional tau restrictions for aggregated GPN
seed_details.open_problem: Give sharp covariate-conditional and aggregated GPN tau-band bounds when the Kendall restriction is conditional on X, constant across X, or only unconditional.

anchor_audit.intro_problem: Chaoge et al. define GPN conditionally on X and then aggregate, so the level at which dependence is restricted matters.
anchor_audit.flagged_open: Copula-envelope formulas are unconditional mathematical objects; the causal question is whether conditional and unconditional tau restrictions yield different sharp aggregated sets.

seed_details.red_team: A technical measurability clean-up, not a new theorem-level object.
seed_details.rebuttal: There is a real identified-set distinction because conditional, constant, and unconditional concordance restrictions are inequivalent and can reverse sharpness ordering after aggregation.
seed_details.step1_verdict: survives, but sits below s1 because the main current file is already locked to the simpler conditional-band kernel.

seed_details.title: Critical tau threshold or set-valued robustness region for majority-causation certification
seed_details.open_problem: Construct uniformly valid inference for the full tau-indexed GPN identified set when endpoint curves have active-bound switches, flat regions, or nonunique crossings.

anchor_audit.intro_problem: Once the tau-indexed set is available, one natural consumer asks when 1/2 enters the identified set.
anchor_audit.flagged_open: The prior repository failures already warned that a scalar monotone robustness threshold need not exist without extra crossing structure.

seed_details.red_team: Falls to the old reviewer objection: no scalar tau* without single-crossing.
seed_details.rebuttal: As a set-valued robustness region it remains meaningful, but the scalar-threshold version is too brittle for the core angle.
seed_details.step1_verdict: dropped as headline kernel; retained only as a downstream consumer of the uniform-band conjecture.

seed_details.title: Population frontier where Gaussian-copula sensitivity coincides with or diverges from the family-free Kendall-band set
seed_details.open_problem: Characterize the family-free sharp GPN identified set obtained by optimizing C(u1,u0) over all copulas whose Kendall's tau lies in a fixed band.

anchor_audit.intro_problem: The anchor paper already uses a Gaussian copula family indexed by dependence.
anchor_audit.flagged_open: A natural comparison asks whether the Gaussian path ever traces the family-free envelope implied by the same Kendall-tau restriction.

seed_details.red_team: Comparator-centric and too narrow; likely just shows strict inclusion generically.
seed_details.rebuttal: Useful as a proposition inside s1, not as a standalone proposal.
seed_details.step1_verdict: dropped as a main angle and retained as the supporting proposition now in the core.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:conditional-tau-band
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The Kendall-tau band is an elicited sensitivity restriction, not identified from the observed law.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The sharp interval claim is accepted only conditional on the maintained conditional Kendall-tau band.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:population-endpoint-eif / thm:regular-one-step-clt
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The EIF and root-n CLT are conditional on active-piece regularity and tailored nuisance-rate assumptions.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The inference rung is accepted as a regular-regime claim, not as a global nonsmooth-endpoint theorem.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:uniform-tau-curve-inference
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Uniform tau-curve inference at active-piece switches is explicitly open.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The tau-uniform coverage claim is accepted only as the proposal's conjectural solve target.

banked_reason: D0.5 repeatedly failed on load-bearing tau-band sharpness, endpoint regularity, and one-step CLT gaps; the last review still required new math beyond an in-place D0.R edit.
